fresh_facts.claim: Israeli airstrikes targeted Hezbollah positions in Lebanon's Beqaa Valley in response to recent attacks, including a drone strike on IDF troops.
US Navy vessel USS Raphael Peralta intercepted Iranian-linked tanker MT Stream en route to an Iranian port as part of a Persian Gulf naval blockade.
The US has conducted 31 naval interception actions against Iranian-linked vessels in the Persian Gulf.
President Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran indefinitely while rejecting Iran's proposal to delay nuclear talks.
Trump issued a 3-day ultimatum to Iran and convened a high-level security meeting to assess military options if diplomacy fails.
Israel seized weapons, drones, and mines during military operations in southern Lebanon as part of border security efforts.
Prime Minister Netanyahu urged the US to maintain the Strait of Hormuz blockade and discussed scenarios for collapsed US-Iran talks.
Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps fired on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz, followed by an attack on a cargo ship.
Approximately half of Iran's ballistic missile stockpile remains intact post-ceasefire.
Iran restarted limited international flights from Tehran despite the conflict, prompting US threats of sanctions against supporting companies.
Iran's Foreign Minister traveled to meet Putin for Russian backing amid stalled talks.
Iran sustains oil exports via shadow fleets and ship-to-ship transfers despite the US-led blockade.
Iran secured a diplomatic win as vice president of the UN Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty Review Conference.
The US is ramping up economic sanctions targeting Iranian airlines and global partners.
President Trump canceled scheduled U.S.-Iran peace talks in Islamabad involving envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner on April 27.
Iran proposed linking the indefinite ceasefire extension to cessation of Israeli aggression in Lebanon.
High-level Iranian officials including Foreign Minister Zarif and Parliament Speaker Qalibaf attended prior diplomatic efforts, signaling regime cohesion.
Reports indicate Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei may be very seriously wounded and injured, though President Rouhani and Foreign Minister Zarif remain in place.
Earlier reports from January 2026 indicated security force refusals to fire on protesters and IRGC concerns over potential defections.
Iran deployed approximately 800-5,000 Iranian-backed Iraqi militias for internal repression in January 2026.
Iran's political stability index reached -1.58 in 2024 data, the weakest on record, declining from -1.27 in 2023.
Prior protest waves in early 2026 involved brutal crackdowns, internet blackouts targeting Starlink, and shoot-to-kill orders from Khamenei issued January 9, 2026.
Security force casualties and financial asset movements by Iranian leaders were observed since January 29, 2026.
German Chancellor criticized the US approach as having no strategy on Iran.
Israel expressed unease over a two-week ceasefire arrangement.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces escalating military pressure from coordinated US-led naval blockade (31 interceptions), Israeli strikes on Hezbollah positions, and IRGC attacks on shipping, while Trump issues a 3-day ultimatum and cancels peace talks despite an indefinite ceasefire extension. Internal regime cohesion shows mixed signals with high-level officials attending diplomatic efforts, but Supreme Leader incapacity, economic sanctions intensification, and shadow fleet operations indicate deepening structural crisis.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 28, 2026, Trump issued a 3-day ultimatum to Iran and convened high-level security meeting on military options while the US Navy executed 31 interception actions against Iranian vessels; simultaneously, Israel conducted strikes on Hezbollah in Lebanon and Iran's IRGC fired on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, marking an escalation across military, diplomatic, and economic dimensions.
